channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Enhancing gas sensing recyclability and recovery rate by crystal engineering of CuI motif in MOFs
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 18 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72117-0

Here authors present a method combining DFT calculations with experimental synthesis to address the challenges of slow recovery and limited recyclability in chemiresistive sensing MOFs.
item.dc:title: Enhancing gas sensing recyclability and recovery rate by crystal engineering of CuI motif in MOFs
item.dc:creator: Zi-Wei Li
Bin Tan
Xiu-Ze Hei
Xing-Wu Liu
Ke Zhao
Chuan-Zhe Wang
Ke-Feng Li
Yong-Jun Chen
Xiao-Ying Huang
Kui Tan
Zhao-Feng Wu
Jing Li
Gang Xu

item.title: Plasmonic modulator enabling kilometer-range high-throughput sub-THz links for radio access networks
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 18 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72053-z

The authors demonstrate a 226-GHz all-photonic wireless link over 1.4 km, achieving a record-high net-rate-distance product of 214.2 Gbits⁻¹ km using broadband photonic/plasmonic components and a dual-sideband receiver that boosts SNR by 2 dB, showing strong turbulence resilience and exciting sub-THz deployment potential.
item.dc:title: Plasmonic modulator enabling kilometer-range high-throughput sub-THz links for radio access networks
item.dc:creator: Boris Vukovic
Laurenz Kulmer
Tobias Blatter
Yannik Horst
Marcel Destraz
Wolfgang Heni
Stefan M. Koepfli
Hande Ibili
Michael Baumann
Yuriy Fedoryshyn
Jasmin Smajic
Sarperi Luciano
Juerg Leuthold
